strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded and high-throughput backend systems, demonstrating breadth required of a CTO
Current Staff Engineer role at re:cinq contributing to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks — directly relevant to the agentic AI mandate
Hands-on fintech and high-stakes commercial exposure via RIDE Capital (German investment platform) and Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, low-latency distributed systems)
concerns: Resume shows individual contributor and staff-level titles rather than explicit CTO/VP Engineering experience scaling teams from 10 to 30+
Agentic AI work described is recent (2025–present) and at framework/tooling level rather than years of production multi-agent system operation
No explicit evidence of board-level communication, hybrid outsourced delivery governance, or running compliance/audit infrastructure across jurisdictions

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic AI and MCP framework experience
Distributed systems and low-latency backend depth

response.justification: The candidate combines rare hands-on agentic AI infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq) with deep distributed-systems credibility from Dunnhumby/Sociomantic's millions-of-requests-per-second platform and direct fintech exposure at RIDE Capital reshaping German investment practices. A 15+ year polyglot track record, founding and operating GISCollective as an event-driven Kubernetes-deployed platform, and a strong craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, public workshops and talks) signal both the technical breadth and the leadership-by-example posture needed to architect a model-agnostic multi-agent fintech stack and shape engineering culture at a venture-backed scale-up.
